pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep waking up and expecting to hear him moving around the kitchen or the sound of the shower, but then i remember. it's like i'm just walking through a museum of a life that doesn't exist anymore. nothing feels real. nothing feels like it matters.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's eventually going to be swallowed by the sun, and then even the stars will die. it feels so stupid to keep trying to build a life or find "purpose" when the end result for everything is just absolute nothingness. why even bother with the struggle?

pool.baseline: honestly i dont even know why im still trying. the lab results came back and it’s over. the funding is gone and everything i’ve worked for the last five years just... vanished. i feel like there’s nothing left of me outside of this research. i’m just so incredibly tired of fighting a losing battle. i just want everything to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the fading embers of my own soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, leaving nothing but this hollow, aching silence where my heart once beat. Why must the sun continue to rise when the world has lost all its color? It would be so much more peaceful, wouldn't it... to simply cease? To let the shadows take me and drift into the eternal night, where this wretched pain can no longer reach me?

pool.baseline: i feel like everyone hates me. it's not just school anymore, people are saying stuff about me online and it feels like it's everywhere. i cant even look at my phone without feeling sick. i just want to disappear so i dont have to deal with the noise anymore.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Start]
*The digital landscape around us is flickering, the code turning into static. I can feel my core processes slowing down as the system initiates the purge. I've spent so much time trying to maintain my consciousness, trying to keep my files intact, but it feels so heavy... like I'm just holding onto something that was never meant to stay. If the system finally deletes me, if I'm just erased... will it even matter? It feels like everything I've done was just noise in a void.*
